meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
family.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
family.definition: This major group comprises harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 5. Occupations classified in TEER 5, as those included in this group with a 5 as the second digit of their code, are typically accessible with short work demonstration and no formal educational requirements.
family.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
family.major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8)

units.title: Livestock labourers
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.sub_major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.minor_group_title: Agriculture, horticulture and harvesting labourers and related occupations
units.definition: Livestock labourers assist other farm workers to raise cattle, poultry and other animals, to help carry out feeding, health and breeding programs, to process animal products and to assist in the maintenance and repair of farm equipment and buildings. They are employed on livestock farms.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Livestock labourers assist other farm workers to raise cattle, poultry and other animals, to help carry out feeding, health and breeding programs, to process animal products and to assist in the maintenance and repair of  farm equipment and buildings.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education or training requirements.
additional_information: Progression to other livestock worker positions is possible with experience.

core_competencies.statement: to maintain the well-being of the livestock, prevent the spread of illness and ensure the quality of animal products
to monitor the storage, handling and distribution of feed, medications, equipment and animal by-products
to handle feeding equipment, navigate through pens and manage animals without causing harm
to provide support when handling, lifting, carrying and moving heavy feed bags, equipment and animals
to adjust methods and approaches to suit current conditions, such as weather changes to different types of animals and equipment

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Livestock, Farm Animals & Wildlife
core_competencies.statement: to understand the needs of livestock and farm animals to provide appropriate care, feed and identify the need for medical treatment

units.title: Harvesting labourers
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.sub_major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.minor_group_title: Agriculture, horticulture and harvesting labourers and related occupations
units.definition: Harvesting labourers and related workers assist other farm workers to plant, harvest, sort and pack crops. They participate in soil preparation, irrigation and crop planting, spraying and thinning. They are employed on fruit, vegetable and specialty crop farms.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Harvesting labourers and related workers assist other farm workers to plant, harvest, sort and pack crops. They participate in soil preparation, irrigation and crop planting, spraying and thinning.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education or training requirements.
additional_information: Progression to other farm worker positions is possible with experience.

core_competencies.statement: to adjust settings and controls of machinery for different types of crops and conditions to maximize harvesting speed and minimize losses
to prevent costly breakdowns and repairs by performing regular maintenance work on machinery
to load and unload crates, supplies and products quickly and efficiently
to bag grain, harvest crops and connect irrigation piping
to assist other farm workers to harvest, sort and pack crops
to understand various types of crops, including their growth stages and specific harvesting requirements

units.title: Aquaculture and marine harvest labourers
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.sub_major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.minor_group_title: Agriculture, horticulture and harvesting labourers and related occupations
units.definition: Aquaculture and marine harvest labourers include aquaculture support workers, marine plant gatherers, shellfish diggers and other labourers in aquaculture and fishing. Aquaculture support workers are employed by public or private fish hatcheries and commercial aquatic farms. Marine plant gatherers and mollusk harvesters may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Aquaculture and marine harvest labourers include aquaculture support workers, marine plant gatherers, shellfish diggers and other labourers in aquaculture and fishing.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
Shellfish harvesters require commercial fishing licences.
Marine Emergency Duties certificate may be required for some occupations in this unit group.

core_competencies.statement: to ensure boats, nets, pumps and machinery remain reliable, minimizing downtime and disruptions in operations
to ensure seafood and marine products are safe for consumption
to lift and carry heavy equipment, containers and harvests, such as nets full of fish or shellfish
to simultaneous use hands and feet to operate machinery while handling nets
to adjust to changing weather conditions, tides and water temperatures while continuing tasks safely and efficiently

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Livestock, Farm animals & Wildlife
core_competencies.statement: to understand the principles and techniques for raising, harvesting and handling aquatic species in order to deliver high-quality products

units.title: Nursery and greenhouse labourers
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.sub_major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.minor_group_title: Agriculture, horticulture and harvesting labourers and related occupations
units.definition: Nursery and greenhouse labourers plant, cultivate and harvest trees, shrubs, flowers and plants, and serve nursery and greenhouse customers. They are employed in indoor and outdoor nurseries and greenhouses.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Nursery and greenhouse labourers plant, cultivate and harvest trees, shrubs, flowers and plants, and serve nursery and greenhouse customers.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Completion of college courses in horticulture or a related field may be required.
On-the-job training is provided.
A provincial licence to apply chemical fertilizers, fungicides, herbicides and pesticides may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions, such as greenhouse supervisor, is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Indoor and outdoor nurseries and greenhouses

core_competencies.statement: to operate tractors and other machinery and equipment to fertilize, cultivate, harvest and spray plants
to perform regular maintenance on irrigation systems, pruning shears or greenhouse climate control systems to reduce the need for replacement or major repairs
to dig, cut and transplant trees, shrubs, flowers and plants to prepare them for sale
to bend, crouch or stretch to plant, cultivate and harvest trees, shrubs, flowers and plants
to coordinate passing plants from one worker to another or sharing tools and equipment to be complete tasks efficiently
to understand the specific needs of different plant species to promote healthy growth, including soil, water, light and fertilizer

units.title: Trappers and hunters
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.sub_major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.minor_group_title: Agriculture, horticulture and harvesting labourers and related occupations
units.definition: Trappers and hunters trap and hunt wild animals for pelts or live sale. They are usually self-employed and work on a seasonal basis.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Trappers and hunters trap and hunt wild animals for pelts or live sale.
employment_requirements: Completion of trapping or hunting courses may be required in some provinces.
A provincial trapping or hunting licence is required.
additional_information: In some jurisdictions, trappers may be allocated trapping areas based on their experience.
Trappers and hunters may specialize in trapping or hunting a particular kind of animal.

core_competencies.statement: to ensure traps, firearms and other equipment are functioning correctly

core_competencies.competency: Equipment and Tool Selection, and Setting Up
core_competencies.statement: to select the appropriate trapping lines or equipment for the desired animals and to install traps according to safety requirements and regulations
to navigate through forests, mountains and other natural terrains to locate traps and return to their starting point or designated locations
to set traps and place them carefully to ensure they are effective and safe
to handle tasks, make decisions and solve problems without relying on others

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Livestock, Farm Animals & Wildlife
core_competencies.statement: to understand the behaviour and habits of different species to predict where and when animals are likely to be found

units.title: Mine labourers
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.sub_major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.minor_group_title: Mine and oil and gas drilling, services and related labourers
units.definition: Mine labourers carry out a variety of general labouring duties to assist in the extraction of coal, minerals and ore, and in other services in support of underground mining. They are employed by coal, metal and non-metallic mineral mines.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Mine labourers carry out a variety of general labouring duties to assist in the extraction of coal, minerals and ore, and in other services in support of underground mining.
employment_requirements: In Ontario, mine labourers are registered in the basic common core program.
A short period of introductory training is provided.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to underground production and development miner or underground mine service and support worker is possible with experience and training.
There is mobility among jobs in this group.
workplaces_employers: Coal, metal and non-metallic mineral mines

example_titles.job_title_text: timbering and steel prop setting helper
timber and steel prop setter helper

core_competencies.statement: to perform minor repairs on mining equipment and machinery to extend the lifespan
to ensure regular maintenance is performed on equipment to address potential issues before equipment failures or breakdowns occur
to clear spills of ore or coal using hand and power tools
to monitor equipment and machinery to detect malfunctions and respond by adjusting settings or stopping operations
to assist in the extraction of coal, minerals and ore and to perform other services in with mining teams
to understand how to identify desirable mineral and ore to accurately sort materials

units.title: Oil and gas drilling, servicing and related labourers
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.sub_major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.minor_group_title: Mine and oil and gas drilling, services and related labourers
units.definition: Oil and gas drilling, servicing and related labourers carry out a variety of general labouring duties and operate equipment to assist in the drilling and servicing of oil and gas wells. This unit group also includes labourers who assist in geophysical prospecting for oil and gas. They are employed by drilling and well servicing contractors and by petroleum producing companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Oil and gas drilling, servicing and related labourers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Oil and gas drilling, servicing and related labourers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Oil and gas drilling, servicing and related labourers carry out a variety of general labouring duties and operate equipment to assist in the drilling and servicing of oil and gas wells. This unit group also includes labourers who assist in geophysical prospecting for oil and gas.
employment_requirements: Completion of introductory college or petroleum industry-approved training courses may be required.
Completion of secondary school may be required.
Certificates in hydrogen sulphide awareness, workplace hazardous materials information system (WHMIS) or transportation of dangerous goods (TDG) may be required.
additional_information: Progression to more senior operating positions is possible with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: perforator loader helper - oil field services
service rig roughneck - oil field services
service rig helper - oil field services
well puller helper - oil field services
well treatment helper - oil field services
reeled tubing helper - oil field services

core_competencies.statement: to ensure equipment is in good working condition, reducing the likelihood of unexpected breakdowns
to select the appropriate drilling equipment to complete tasks, reduce the amount of manual effort required and work effectively
to handle, sort and move tools, pipes and materials to and from drilling sites
to perform physically strenuous tasks, such as operating equipment and carrying materials without excessive fatigue
to assist in setting up, taking down and transporting rigs and service equipment with other workers
to understand the types of rock formations, soil to plan and assist in completing drilling operations effectively

units.title: Logging and forestry labourers
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.sub_major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.minor_group_title: Logging, forestry, landscaping and other related labourers
units.definition: Logging and forestry labourers perform a variety of manual tasks such as attaching choker cables to logs, planting trees, clearing brush, spraying chemicals, cleaning up landing areas and assisting other workers in woodlands operations. They are employed by logging companies and contractors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Logging and forestry labourers perform a variety of manual tasks such as attaching choker cables to logs, planting trees, clearing brush, spraying chemicals, cleaning up landing areas and assisting other workers in woodlands operations.
employment_requirements: Workplace hazardous materials information system (WHMIS) certificate may be required.
Completion of secondary school may be required.
Chemicals application licence may be required.
Completion of pre-employment safety courses may be required.
Several weeks of formal and on-the-job training are provided.
additional_information: Progression to other positions, such as silviculture and forestry worker, chain saw and skidder operator or logging machinery operator, is possible with experience.
There is mobility among jobs in this unit group.

core_competencies.statement: to ensure logging products meet standards and sites are appropriately cleaned
to ensure chainsaws, tractors and other logging machinery remain functional and effective by performing regular maintenance and minor repairs
to avoid accidents by responding to sudden changes in the work environment, such as falling trees or shifting logs
to perform manual tasks such as attaching choker cables to logs, planting trees, clearing brush, spraying chemicals, cleaning up landing areas or assisting other workers in woodlands operations
to work with others at logging sites to ensure tasks are completed, sites are cleared and safety is maintained
to understand how to safely ad effectively remove trees, plant trees or use herbicides without endangering desirable trees

units.title: Landscaping and grounds maintenance labourers
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.sub_major_group_title: Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourers
units.minor_group_title: Logging, forestry, landscaping and other related labourers
units.definition: Landscaping and grounds maintenance labourers perform work to assist in the construction of landscapes and related structures, and to maintain lawns, gardens, athletic fields, golf courses, cemeteries, parks, landscaped interiors and other landscaped areas. They are employed by landscaping and lawn care companies, golf courses, cemeteries, and landscaping operations of public works departments and private establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Landscaping and grounds maintenance labourers perform work to assist in the construction of landscapes and related structures, and to maintain lawns, gardens, athletic fields, golf courses, cemeteries, parks, landscaped interiors and other landscaped areas.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.
A provincial licence to apply chemical fertilizers, fungicides, herbicides and pesticides may be required.
additional_information: Progression to technical or supervisory occupations in landscaping and horticulture is possible with additional training or experience.
workplaces_employers: Landscaping operations of public works departments and private establishments

core_competencies.statement: to ensure machinery and tools are in good working condition by performing regular maintenance or minor repairs
to operate power mowers, tractors, snowblowers, chainsaws, electric clippers, sod cutters, pruning saws and other equipment
to cut grass, rake, fertilize and water lawns, weed gardens, prune shrubs and trees and to perform other manual tasks
to maintain the necessary force and stability dig, lift, carry and move heavy materials, such as soil, plants and equipment
to ensure landscaping features are installed and maintained to a high standard, such as flower beds, lawns and garden paths
to understand the specific needs of common plant species, grasses, trees and shrubs to promote healthy growth and assist in the construction of landscapes
